errors.message:
Cloning into '/Users/ghh/Documents/Code/Code2MCP-private/workspace/pymatgen/temp_clone'...
fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/materialsproject/pymatgen/': LibreSSL SSL_connect: SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL in connection to github.com:443

warnings:
git clone failed: Cloning into '/Users/ghh/Documents/Code/Code2MCP-private/workspace/pymatgen/temp_clone'...
fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/materialsproject/pymatgen/': LibreSSL SSL_connect: SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL in connection to github.com:443
recommendations: add a minimal smoke test suite in `mcp_output/tests_mcp` that imports the plugin and exercises 3–5 high-value endpoints (`MPRester`
`Structure`
`PhaseDiagram`
`PourbaixDiagram`
`Vasprun`)
add CI for the MCP plugin (lint + tests) instead of relying only on upstream pymatgen workflows
fix dependency detection mismatch (analysis says no `pyproject/requirements` while files exist) to improve adapter generation reliability
pin and validate compatible version ranges for heavy scientific deps (`numpy/scipy/spglib/pandas/matplotlib`) in `mcp_output/requirements.txt`
add graceful optional-dependency guards for `mp-api`
`openbabel`
`rdkit`
`vtk`
and `phonopy` with actionable error messages
define explicit MCP endpoint schemas (input/output Pydantic models) for each exposed class/function to avoid ambiguous tool contracts
reduce exposed surface by curating endpoints to stable/public APIs only (avoid “legacy helper” internals)
add request timeouts/retries/backoff for external API clients (`MPRester`
`OptimadeRester`) to improve robustness
implement structured error mapping in `adapter.py`/`mcp_service.py` (validation vs dependency vs runtime vs upstream API errors)
add caching for expensive/repeated computations (phase diagram construction
repeated structure parsing) with cache invalidation controls
add payload size limits and streaming/chunking strategy for very large structures/JSON test assets
add observability (per-endpoint latency
error rate
payload size logs) and a debug flag in `start_mcp.py`
document supported Python/version matrix and optional feature flags in `README_MCP.md`
add security hardening: secret handling for MP API keys
input sanitization for file-path based parsers
and safe defaults for network access
create golden-file contract tests for serialization round-trips of `Structure/Molecule/Lattice/Composition` to ensure stable MCP outputs
split large monolithic analysis modules into focused adapter wrappers to lower complexity and improve maintainability
add a deprecation policy and semantic versioning plan for MCP endpoint names
include quick benchmark scripts for key endpoints and set baseline performance thresholds in CI

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal success state and executed all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Robust fallback behavior: after git clone SSL failure, pipeline continued via zip fallback and still analyzed ~504 files.
Import-based adapter strategy matched repository characteristics (high import feasibility: 0.88, low intrusiveness risk).
Generated MCP service passed plugin health/startup checks over stdio transport.
failure_reasons: Primary repository acquisition failed due to TLS/network issue (LibreSSL SSL_connect SYSCALL to github.com:443).
Quality signals are partially unreliable because observability metrics are zero/unknown (LLM calls, deepwiki calls, CPU/memory, generated LOC).
Dependency detection subsystem produced false negatives (reported no pyproject/requirements despite files present), risking incomplete generation.
Original project tests were not actually validated (passed=false with empty details and no test files).

node_performance.download_time: Initial clone failed quickly; recovery via zip fallback enabled continued execution. Download node behavior is resilient but network-fragile.
node_performance.analysis_time: Moderate-to-heavy analysis implied by 504-file scan and module identification across large scientific codebase.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation completed successfully and emitted core MCP scaffold files; endpoint metadata suggests broad surface exposure.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin smoke health passed, but upstream/original test validation effectively absent; test depth is insufficient.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient data (reported 0 MB). Cannot confirm runtime memory profile.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient data (reported 0%). No CPU efficiency conclusion possible.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Likely efficient for generation itself, but metrics are missing (generated size reported 0 despite files created), indicating telemetry defects.
